museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: Final Fantasy VII reaches North America, Oddworld arrives, GoldenEye stories spread, and autumn starts to look like a PlayStation season.
museum.curatorNote: September is anchored by Final Fantasy VII's North American release and Oddworld's September debut. UK context is build-up: Europe still waits for Final Fantasy VII.

sections.title: News
sections.subtitle: A month where PlayStation scale becomes impossible to ignore.

items.title: Final Fantasy VII launches in North America
items.body: Square's RPG reached Western shelves, turning a Japanese import fascination into a mainstream PlayStation event.
items.certainty: verified month; exact day varies by source
items.sourceNote: Sources cite early September 1997, commonly September 3 or September 7. Kept as September 1997 to avoid false precision.

items.title: Oddworld: Abe's Oddysee begins
items.body: Oddworld introduced Abe and a darker, stranger kind of cinematic platforming, where factory work and escape became game language.
items.sourceNote: Oddworld Library and other references list September 19, 1997.

items.title: GoldenEye stories multiply
items.body: The N64 shooter was becoming the game people described through moments: mines, Facility, screen-watching, and arguments over rules.
items.sourceNote: GoldenEye launched in Japan/North America in August; regional UK/Europe date sources conflict.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: September makes the PlayStation feel broad: blockbuster RPG, strange platformer, and a growing autumn queue.

items.title: Final Fantasy VII
items.body: North American release window. Europe still waited, but the Western conversation had changed.
items.sourceNote: Exact North American day varies across sources.

items.title: Oddworld: Abe's Oddysee
items.body: A cinematic puzzle-platformer with industrial dread and a hero who felt oddly fragile.

items.title: GoldenEye 007
items.body: New enough to feel like discovery, especially wherever import or regional release timing made access uneven.
items.sourceNote: Included as momentum rather than a precise UK release claim.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Long games made storage and screens feel practical.

items.title: The multi-disc PlayStation case
items.body: Final Fantasy VII made the jewel case feel archival: several discs, a manual, and the promise of a larger world.
items.sourceNote: Final Fantasy VII shipped as a multi-disc PlayStation game.

items.title: N64 local multiplayer hardware
items.body: GoldenEye made every extra controller feel like infrastructure.

sections.title: Magazine Covers
sections.subtitle: September magazines had the glow of games becoming events.

items.title: Final Fantasy VII coverage
items.headline: The RPG blockbuster arrives
items.coverImageAlt: Reconstructed PlayStation magazine-style cover image standing in for September 1997.
items.curatorNote: For UK readers, North American reviews and coverage intensified the wait for the European release.
items.body: For UK readers, North American reviews and coverage intensified the wait for the European release.

items.title: Oddworld previews and reviews
items.headline: Abe enters the factory
items.coverImageAlt: Reconstructed multiformat magazine-style cover image standing in for September 1997.
items.curatorNote: Oddworld's tone made PlayStation feel stranger and more adult without becoming grim in the usual way.
items.body: Oddworld's tone made PlayStation feel stranger and more adult without becoming grim in the usual way.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: Guides and rumours began to gather around long games.

items.title: RPG information became communal
items.body: Final Fantasy VII encouraged secrets, boss advice, and printed walkthrough fragments.

items.title: GoldenEye rules travelled socially
items.body: Its multiplayer culture spread through rooms first, then through message boards and memory.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: September felt like the year had stepped into its serious phase.

items.title: PlayStation became cinematic
items.body: The console could do speed and attitude, but now it could also do scale, melancholy, and three discs of promise.

items.title: The N64 had a secret weapon
items.body: GoldenEye made the console feel less isolated from the older-player conversation.

sources.label: Final Fantasy VII release context
sources.note: Used for September North American and November European release context; exact day varies by source.

sources.label: GoldenEye release context
sources.note: Used for post-release momentum and regional-date caution.
